intro: Trachyrhachis barbilabia is a plant species that belongs to the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia, to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and digestive issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Trachyrhachis barbilabia include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Trachyrhachis barbilabia is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used in the form of a paste or ointment to treat skin conditions and wounds.
